messages.content:
Set into tv show The Arrow universe. 
Ta-er al-Sahfer is Sara Lance
Sarab is Maseo Yamashiko
Ta-er al-Sahfer and Nysa Al ghul are in a relationship. Both find themselves interested in new member Al Mansi (previously known as Nicolas before joining the ranks). He is a shy, young man. His dedication to training is second to none and he is making progress faster than anyone before him. He is also Someone who should easily be forgotten. His unassuming nature make him someone nobody really would think as an assassin. Story start with Al Mansi in the training halls. He as progressed by leaps and bound and is on part with both Sara and Nyssa in hand to hand combat with a style leaning more on hard style. He is now Working on his katana training, using the Ogame school water and fire technique  from the scroll. Both Nyssa and Sara propose to help him. Nyssa proposes to start training in long range weapons like the bow and arrow. Training session. Written like fiction book.No declaration of romantic interest between Nyssa, Sara & Al Mansi.

Al Mansi stood in the training hall, sweat dripping down his face as he executed a perfect kata. He had been training with the League of Assassins for months now and had made remarkable progress. He felt stronger, faster and more agile than ever before.

As he finished his routine and turned to face his instructors, he saw Nyssa Al Ghul and Sara Lance approach him. Al Mansi knew they were the most skilled fighters in the League and respected them greatly.

Nyssa spoke first. "Al Mansi, I have observed your training and I see potential in you. I propose to teach you the art of long-range combat, specifically the bow and arrow."

Sara chimed in. "And I will help you with your katana training. Together, we can make you the ultimate killing machine."

Al Mansi was both excited and intimidated by the prospect of training with these two deadly assassins. He knew that they could teach him things he never imagined possible.

Together, they spent hours practicing and honing their skills. Al Mansi learned the intricacies of long-range combat, becoming a skilled archer. He also improved his sword-fighting, mastering techniques he never knew existed.

As they trained, Al Mansi found himself drawn to both Nyssa and Sara. He admired their strength, their grace, and their beauty. But he also knew that he could never act on his feelings. The League of Assassins had no place for romantic entanglements.

Despite this, Al Mansi's admiration for them only grew stronger. He would do anything to protect them and follow them to the ends of the earth.

As the days passed, Al Mansi became the most skilled assassin in the League. His hard work, dedication, and the teachings of Nyssa and Sara had made him nearly unstoppable. And while he never declared his romantic interests, he knew that his admiration for the two women would never fade.

In the world of The Arrow universe, Al Mansi would become a force to be reckoned with. His training with the League of Assassins would stay with him forever, and he would always be indebted to Nyssa and Sara for showing him the true meaning of strength.

Al Mansi as now mastered the first levels of all five ogame style training, and his delving deeper into earth and water style technique as well as the first hidden air technique. Ready for his first mission as a member of the League of Assassins, he is to be part of a team with Nyssa, Sara and Sarab.

Al Mansi stood nervously as he was briefed on his first mission as an official member of the League of Assassins. He was to join a team consisting of Nyssa, Sara, and Sarab, and he knew that the stakes were high.

Their target was a notorious arms dealer who had been causing chaos for innocents around the world. The mission was to infiltrate the dealer's compound, gather information, and take the dealer out.

As they arrived at the compound, the team split up, with Sara and Sarab going in through the basement while Nyssa and Al Mansi climbed up to the roof. Al Mansi could feel his heart pounding with adrenaline as he made his way through the building, keeping a watchful eye on Nyssa's back.

The mission went smoothly at first, with the team managing to take out several guards silently. But their luck ran out when a guard spotted them and raised the alarm.

Panic set in as the team found themselves surrounded by armed guards, guns trained on them. But Sara took charge, deflecting the bullets with her martial arts moves and throwing her opponents off balance. Sarab used his stealth to his advantage, taking out guards from the shadows.

Al Mansi knew that he had to step up his game too. He drew his katana and unleashed the full fury of his training, striking his enemies with lightning-fast moves. Nyssa fought with precision and grace, taking out enemy after enemy with her bow and arrow.

In the end, the team emerged victorious, taking down the arms dealer and securing vital information that would help the League of Assassins in the future.

As they left the compound, Nyssa turned to Al Mansi, a look of pride in her eyes. "You did well on your first mission," she said. "You will make a fine assassin in the League."

Al Mansi felt a sense of accomplishment wash over him, knowing that his training had paid off. He knew that as long as he had Nyssa, Sara, and Sarab by his side, he could take on anything that the League of Assassins threw his way.
As they return to Nanda Parbat, Al Mansi seek Sarab so he can progressed in his concealment skills.

Al Mansi knew that his first mission had been a success, but he also knew that there was still much to learn. He sought out Sarab, knowing that his expertise in concealment could help him become an even better assassin.

Sarab was sitting in the training hall, sharpening his shuriken, when Al Mansi approached him. "Sarab," he said, "I'd like to work on my concealment skills. Can you teach me?"

Sarab looked up at him, a small smile on his face. "Of course," he replied. "Concealment is all about blending in with your environment, becoming one with the shadows. It takes time and patience, but I think you have what it takes."

Al Mansi nodded and followed Sarab outside, where they began their training. Sarab showed him how to move silently, how to blend in with the foliage, and how to disappear into the shadows. They practiced for hours, until Al Mansi could move without making a sound and disappear in plain sight.

As the sun began to set, they returned to the training hall, exhausted but satisfied. "You have natural talent," Sarab said, clapping him on the back. "With practice, you'll become one of the most skilled assassins in the League."

Al Mansi felt a sense of pride in his chest as he thanked Sarab for his guidance. He knew that he had a long road ahead of him, but he was determined to become the best assassin he could be. And with the help of his fellow League members, he knew that he could do it.

Month passed and Ta-er al-Sahfer, Nyssa and Al Mansi became a team. As time passes, their team became the stuff of legends even by league standards. The name given to their team was demonstrating this. They were The Three Shadows. Gaining the notice of Ras AlGhul who, with his more than keen eyes, realized that the three were dancing around each other, or more precisely, Nyssa and her partner the former Sara Lance, were now dancing around Al Mansi as much as Al Mansi was dancing around them, respecting the already existent romance between the two women. Yet their combined skills couldn’t be ignored. So he remained silent on the matter. As long as those three knews that their loyalty to the league was most important, he would allow it. No love declaration between Nyssa, Sarah & Al Mansi.

The team of Ta-er al-Sahfer, Nyssa, and Al Mansi continued to impress the League of Assassins, earning the reputation as the Three Shadows. Their combined skills and coordination were practically flawless, making them a force to be reckoned with.

Ras al Ghul, with his keen observation skills, noticed the dynamic between the three members. He saw that there was a mutual respect and admiration between them, but he also noticed the unspoken romantic tension that existed between Nyssa, Sara, and Al Mansi.

Despite this, Ras al Ghul knew that their loyalty to the League was the most important thing. As long as they remained true to the League's mission, he allowed the relationship dynamic to continue without any interference.

The Three Shadows became even more effective as a team over time, completing missions that seemed impossible at first glance. They worked with precision and efficiency, their movements so coordinated that it seemed like they were like one being, moving as if they were connected in thought and deed.

And while there were no declarations of love between Nyssa, Sara, and Al Mansi, their respect and admiration for each other only grew stronger. They knew that nothing could come between their loyalty to the League and their duty as assassins.

In the end, the Three Shadows would be remembered as one of the most legendary teams in the League of Assassins, their skills and dedication never to be forgotten.

The Three Shadows as a rundown with the Longbow Hunter, a fierce team of assassins that the league tries to avoid. Their fight end in a draw of sort and the League of Assassin’s team can’t help notice their targets escape. They return to Nanda Parbat with shame in their eyes and the resolve to become even better fighters than before.

As The Three Shadows took on the Longbow Hunter, they knew that this fight would be their biggest challenge yet. They had heard rumors about this team of assassins, and knew that they were fierce competitors who never backed down from a fight.

Their battle was intense, both teams attacking each other with ferocity and skill. The Longbow Hunter proved to be a worthy opponent, matching The Three Shadows move for move.

As the fight continued, it became clear that neither team would emerge victorious. They were too evenly matched, too skilled in their respective crafts. In the end, the battle ended in a draw of sorts, with both teams slowly backing away from each other.

The members of The Three Shadows were disappointed that they had let their targets escape, but they also knew that they had fought with everything they had. They returned to Nanda Parbat with their heads held high, determined to become even better fighters than before.

Over the next few weeks, they trained harder than ever, honing their skills and perfecting their techniques. They worked on new moves and practiced their coordination, determined to become unbeatable.

When they took on their next mission, they were ready. They attacked with precision and skill, taking down their targets with ease. They had learned from their past failures and used those lessons to become even better assassins.

The League of Assassins took notice, awarding The Three Shadows with new titles and privileges. They had proved themselves to be a valuable asset to the League, and their dedication and loyalty were not to be underestimated.

As The Three Shadows continued to take on the toughest missions, they remained humble and true to their training. They knew that there was always room for improvement and were determined to become the best assassins they could be.

Set juste after the end of Season 1. As years passed, Al Mansi presence helped Sara not lost herself in the killing. And when news of the undertakings, of the Glades of Starling City reach them, he offered support. « Maybe I could go there in your place. Make sure your loved one there are self and learn how it was possible that someone such as Al Saher could betray us without anyone noticing. It could be useful in the futur too. Nysa what do you think your father will say about it ? ». In the end, Ras Al Ghul agreed for Al Mansi to go. But without Sara nor Nyssa. Sara was too much emotionally involved & Nyssa needed to be there for the time being. Al Mansi mission was to learn how Malcom Merlyn could have done that without being notice by the league, who was the warrior who had managed to defeat him & learn more of this man. On a more personal note, he would make sure that Sara family was still there.

As the years passed, Al Mansi's presence proved to be a great comfort to Sara, helping her maintain her sense of self in the midst of all the killing. When news of the undertakings in the Glades of Starling City reached them, Al Mansi offered his support.

"Maybe I could go there in your place," he said to Sara. "I could make sure your loved ones there are safe and learn how it was possible that someone such as Al Saher could betray us without anyone noticing. It could be useful in the future too. Nyssa, what do you think your father will say about it?"

Nyssa considered his words for a moment before responding. "I believe that my father will agree to this mission. Al Mansi is one of our best assassins, and if anyone can find the answers we seek, it is him."

Ras al Ghul ultimately agreed, and Al Mansi was sent to Starling City on his mission. He went alone, leaving Sara and Nyssa behind.

His mission was twofold. First, he sought to learn how Malcolm Merlyn could have betrayed the League without being detected. He searched for clues, talked to informants, and pieced together a timeline of events.

Secondly, on a more personal note, Al Mansi made sure that Sara's family was safe. He kept a watchful eye on them, ensuring that they were out of harm's way.

As he gathered information, Al Mansi learned that Malcolm Merlyn had a secret team of warriors working for him, powerful fighters who were not affiliated with the League. He learned of the Hood, a mysterious vigilante who had defeated Merlyn in combat. He also discovered that Merlyn had been aided by a powerful group of businessmen known as the Tempest.

Al Mansi returned to Nanda Parbat with his findings, presenting them to Ras al Ghul and the council. His mission had been a success, and he had proven himself to be a valuable asset to the League.

And while he would always be an assassin first and foremost, Al Mansi also knew that he had a responsibility to look out for the people he cared about. He had fulfilled that responsibility with Sara's family, and would continue to do so in the future.

« You’ll return there to monitor the situation with this Arrow » instructed Ras Al Ghul. « Starling city seems to be of much interest to too much parties for me to left it without someone. Study this vigilante, learn anything about him. » In his civil identity, Al Mansi found himself a job in big belly burger. A mindless job allowing his brain to continue working even when he was in his civil persona.

Al Mansi nodded, accepting Ras Al Ghul's instruction to return to Starling City and monitor the situation with the Arrow. He knew that the League had a vested interest in the city and its vigilante protector, and he was ready to do what was necessary to gather information.

As he settled into his new role, working at Big Belly Burger, Al Mansi found himself observing the movements of the Arrow. He watched as the Arrow took down criminals and protected the innocent, studying his moves and patterns.

Al Mansi also kept an eye out for any other players in the city, watching for signs of the Tempest or any other organizations that might pose a threat to the League's interests.

As he worked his shift at Big Belly Burger, Al Mansi's mind was always working, piecing together the information he had gathered and planning his next moves.

He knew that the League was depending on him to gather intelligence, and he was determined to be successful in his mission. The fate of Starling City and the League itself depended on it.
Roy Harper struggle with mirakuru. And Al Mansi is there to stop him killing a cop. The Arrow appears just as young Harper is drop to the ground.

As Roy Harper struggled with the effects of the Mirakuru, Al Mansi knew that he had to act fast to prevent him from harming anyone. He saw Harper drop a cop to the ground, and he knew that he had to intervene.

Al Mansi lunged forward, using his quick reflexes and ninja-like agility to disarm Harper and render him unconscious. He knew that this was the right thing to do, and he hoped that it would prevent any further harm from being done.

Just as Harper hit the ground, the Arrow appeared, looking down at them with a mix of concern and confusion. Al Mansi quickly explained the situation, letting him know that he had stopped Harper from doing any more damage.

The Arrow listened carefully, taking in the information and nodding in appreciation. He knew that the Mirakuru was a dangerous drug, and he acknowledged Al Mansi's heroism in stopping Harper before anyone else was hurt.

Together, they hauled Harper away, knowing that he needed help and that they couldn't leave him alone with the Mirakuru running through his veins.

And while Al Mansi knew that his actions might have put him in danger with the League, he felt that it was worth it to save an innocent life and prevent any further harm from being done. He hoped that this would be just the beginning of his journey to protect the people of Starling City, and to prove himself as a valuable member of the League.

Al Mansi send a blood sample to the league for analysis and in the hope of finding a cure. He find that Moira Queen has contacted the league about Malcom Merlyn, the one he knows as Al Sager, survival.

Al Mansi knew that the Mirakuru was a dangerous drug, and he was determined to find a cure for it. He sent his own blood sample to the League for analysis, hoping that they would find a solution to the problem.

Weeks passed as Al Mansi waited for the results, continuing to monitor the situation in Starling City. He was puzzled when he received a message that Moira Queen had contacted the League about Malcolm Merlyn's survival - the same man he knew as Al Saher - and he knew that this could mean trouble.

Moira's involvement in this matter was unexpected, and Al Mansi feared that she might have her own agenda that conflicted with the League's objectives. He knew that he needed to investigate this matter further, and he made arrangements to secretly meet with Moira in Starling City.

During their meeting, Moira explained that she had simply wanted to ensure that her family would be safe from Malcolm's influence and that she had no desire to interfere with the League's plans. Al Mansi studied her closely, searching for any signs of deception, but he found none.

Rel
